plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL entirely to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim QQQI to 15% for a balanced income component.
Increase QQQ to 20% to maintain broad market exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
This plan aims for capital preservation and reduced overall risk.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5% per month return, prioritizing capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, leading to lower expected drawdown.

moves.note: Reduce leveraged ETF to small size

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell SOXL completely as it is flagged and highly leveraged.
Reduce SPAL to 5% for minimal leveraged exposure.
Trim QQQI to 15% to balance income with growth.
Buy ANET at 5% for its strong risk/reward and quality.
Buy NOW at 5% as a top-scoring scanner setup.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while introducing high-quality growth names, balancing potential upside with moderate drawdown risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its sell flag and significant loss.
Reduce QQQI to 10% to shift focus towards growth.
Trim SMH to 10% due to its downtrend status.
Buy ANET at 10% to lean into a high risk/reward setup.
Buy NOW at 10% as a top-scoring growth opportunity.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month by leaning into high-scoring setups and using leverage. This approach significantly increases both potential upside and drawdown risk, with leveraged ETFs subject to daily decay and rebalancing. Strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% for a core broad index holding.
Sell SOXL, SPAL, SMH, and CRWD to eliminate high-beta and leveraged exposure.
Significantly increase QQQI to 40% for enhanced monthly distributions.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to shift away from growth focus.
This plan prioritizes steady income and low drawdown.
Maintain a substantial cash buffer for stability.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% per month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. It achieves this by rotating into income-focused ETFs and significantly reducing high-beta and leveraged holdings. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market downturns.
